mentions.note: Down from $233; shifted model from pure middleman to service manager; filling gap for independent medical orgs; high growth trading <20x earnings; vicious healthcare rotation created steal; been buying for charitable trust.

Trades 11x earnings with terrific quarter from CEO David Joyner; owns 8,932 drugstores; only two competitors left (Rite Aid bankrupt, Walgreens private); upside from disappearing competition; preferred to ServiceNow.
Avoid pseudo fintech; prefer energy bars; sell fintech position.

Up 65% YTD from sixth-worst performer; solid Q1 (revenue beat, smaller loss); Strait of Hormuz closure creating record chemical pricing power (10-30 cents/lb uplift); supply delays last through 2026-2027; Q2 guidance $12B revenue, $2B EBITDA with upside; tracking $6B annualized EBITDA well above consensus.

Rate-cut template stock since 2007 (present since Elmont, Long Island 1987); owns during rate cuts; provides portfolio balance against software/SaaS exposure; get rate cuts with Home Depot, not Intel.
Triple-A balance sheet (one of only two, other being MSFT); best pipeline of potential blockbusters of any pharma; trades <20x earnings; quality healthcare at discount.
Preferred rare earth exposure over speculative Critical Metals; for real in rare earth sector.

Excellent quarter (Rule of 50 pass, strong AI adoption) but down 44% YTD; enterprise software pricing under pressure from AI; trading 37x earnings even after 17% crash; won't get premium multiple in disruption era.

Q1 beat; lowered CA residential rates 23% (vulnerable customers) while maintaining growth; 4.5 GW data center demand in final engineering, 10+ GW pre-application; serves AWS, Google Cloud, Microsoft; fifth year of double-digit earnings growth with rate reductions; single-issue stock on wildfire liability reform; deserves $25, not $17.
Biggest decliner in healthcare group; still too expensive; GOP healthcare threat existential to stock.
Warming up after last quarter was good; Cramer surprised; wife considering purchase; looks like they'll make it; stock higher if they execute.

Solid quarter (revenue beat, 19-cent EPS beat, full-year guidance raised substantially); organic growth 1% but pharma/biotech (60% revenue) mid-single-digit with strengthening orders; biotech funding flowing, AI-enabled discovery via Nvidia collab driving autonomous labs; market has reaction wrong.
Cramer likes it; those who don't like Tesla probably short Intel too; robots and full self-driving are revolutionary.

First solid beat-and-raise in long time; CEO Steve Hemsley (juggernaut builder) returned; best managed care earnings in ages; stock trades 19x despite strength; was $600 in April 2025; group hated but Hemsley just getting started; deserves much higher valuation.
